data.body: Implemented the 4 clearly-valid drift fixes: api-stability.md 0.6.0->0.7.1; event-schema.md ticket_txn.py->_commands/txn.py; README CLI block gains 'rebar idea' + 'rebar explain'; new tests/unit/test_version_sync.py asserts api-stability.md 'currently X.Y.Z' == pyproject.toml version (a unit test = CI guard that gates on the change's own tree, no workflow-bootstrap lag). DIVERGED from 'delete docs/bash-migration.md': the plan-review flagged (valid) that the ticket's 'no inbound links' premise is false — bash-migration.md has ~14 inbound refs incl 9 production-source module docstrings (validate.py, next_batch.py, lock.py, composer.py, leaf.py, unlink.py, _seam.py, _lib_writes.py, _commands/__init__.py cite its section numbers) plus README:604 and docs/archive/*. Deleting it would orphan all those citations, so it is kept as the historical migration record. config.py:442 'Release: 0.6.0' marker was already removed by 7c92, so that item is a no-op.
